identity.name: Chan Meng
identity.tagline: Building at the intersection of AI, cultural technology, and women's health.
identity.philosophy: Subtraction for life, addition for thought.
identity.voiceSummary: Architecture-grade vocabulary, evidence-first claims, restraint as a statement. Cross-cultural fluency without translation.
identity.signaturePhrase: Subtraction for life, addition for thought.
audiences: Founders evaluating an AI agent architect / fractional CTO
Recruiters and hiring managers in AI / full-stack / FemTech
Engineers and learners following the open-source craft
Te Ao Māori partners and cultural-technology collaborators

displaySerif.role: Hero displays, pull-quotes — at most once per page.
displaySerif.licenseNote: Fraunces is OFL — free for web embed and self-host.

bodySans.role: Body text, headings h2-h3, UI labels — the workhorse.
bodySans.licenseNote: Inter is OFL. Segoe UI is the Windows fallback (system font, no license risk).

mono.role: Data signatures — datestamps, paths, version numbers, code.
mono.licenseNote: JetBrains Mono is OFL. Consolas is the Windows fallback.

display.note: Hero only. At most once per page.

h1.note: Major section headings on long-form surfaces.

h2.note: Section headers (CV/README — preserves existing CV behaviour).

body.note: Default body. Never below this size.

eyebrow.note: The ONLY uppercased element in the system.

principles: Eyebrow is the ONLY uppercased element in the system.
Display serif appears at most once per page (hero, OR pull-quote, never both).
Mono is reserved for data signatures — datestamps, paths, version numbers, code.
Body never sits below 16px on web or 9pt in print.
Negative tracking on display only (never on body).

monogram.note: Used as section divider mark — see signatures.monogramDivider.

doNot: Do not recolor the logo — black on warm paper is canonical.
Do not place the logo on photographic backgrounds without a paper mat.
Do not skew, rotate, or apply effects (shadows, glows, gradients).
Do not pair the logo with a generated wordmark — the SVG IS the wordmark.

principles.name: Architecture-grade vocabulary
principles.rule: Prefer engineering nouns (orchestration, dispatch, hub-and-spoke) over marketing adjectives (powerful, robust, world-class).
principles.why: Chan's existing CV uses precise nouns; recruiters who matter recognise them.

principles.name: Evidence-first
principles.rule: Every claim links to one proof point (PR, repo, live URL, paper). Adjectives without links get cut.
principles.why: Established by the README redesign spec; carry forward.

principles.name: Subtraction for life, addition for thought
principles.rule: At most one gold mark per visual unit; remove decoration until what is left is the idea.
principles.why: Chan's stated personal philosophy; the brand must enact it.

principles.name: Cross-cultural fluency without translation
principles.rule: Te reo Māori, 中文, English appear in their own forms — italicised once, then treated as first-class vocabulary. No parenthetical glosses unless the audience demonstrably needs them.
principles.why: Existing CV does this with 'Te Whare Tapa Whā'; codify.

principles.name: Pace by white space
principles.rule: Visual rhythm beats density. The 61.8/38.2 ratio in the CV is canonical; default to it elsewhere too.
principles.why: Existing theme.typ already chose this; promote it from CV-only to system-wide.

principles.name: Never apologise for restraint
principles.rule: No 'sorry it's plain', no 'minimal-but-' qualifiers. The restraint is the statement.
principles.why: Restraint loses its meaning the moment it is hedged.

do: Recruited by Anthropic for the Claude residency programme.
86.6% solo-built; AI mentor matching and membership-flow automation in production.
Replaces click-through workflows with agentic ones: natural-language intent drives downstream orchestration.
dont: Leveraged cutting-edge AI to deliver robust, world-class solutions.
I'm a passionate, results-driven full-stack rockstar.

heroBand.doNotUseWhen: Internal sections. Multiple per page. Cards.

goldEyebrow.rule: eyebrow scale + accent.editorial color + ' — ' (em-dash, not en-dash) before display heading
goldEyebrow.useWhen: Above any section that opens with display serif. Above the hero subhead.
goldEyebrow.doNotUseWhen: Inline body. After the section already used a gold mark this page.

monoDatestamp.rule: mono scale 13px in ink.muted; format: 'last updated · YYYY-MM-DD · build N'
monoDatestamp.useWhen: Footer of every long-form surface (README, CV, brand-system, future webpages).

monogramDivider.useWhen: Between major narrative shifts (e.g. between 'Featured work' and 'Open source').
monogramDivider.doNotUseWhen: Between sibling cards or list items.

pillRotation.doNotUseWhen: Inside body prose. As decoration. Outside Chan's own tools.

freeTools.text: Free tools I built for the community

imagery.direction: Editorial restraint. Real assets over stock. When portrait photography appears, prefer working-context over posed. When project screenshots appear, frame them in a warm-paper mat with hairline rule — let the UI be protagonist.
approved: /public/tech-logos/ (third-party tech stack marks — used in pill-row context only)
forbidden: Trophy / shields.io / wakatime / GitHub-streak badges (external decoration services)

readme.canvas: GitHub renders white; warm-paper canvas applies only to images we control
readme.fontHint: GitHub controls typography — semantic tokens here are advisory for img/SVG only

socialCard.note: Square / 1.91:1 export. Eyebrow + display serif only.
